MLB World Series odds 2025

It's never too early to start looking at the MLB World Series odds; here's a breakdown of the top contenders as we move through the 2024-25 offseason (odds updated Feb. 5):

TeamOddsImplied ProbabilityProfit on $10 Bet
Los Angeles Dodgers+25028.57%$25
New York Yankees+75011.76%$75
Atlanta Braves+90010.00%$90
Philadelphia Phillies+11008.33%$110
New York Mets+12007.69%$120
Baltimore Orioles+14006.67%$140
Houston Astros+21004.55%$210
Texas Rangers+24004.00%$240
Seattle Mariners+26003.70%$260
San Diego Padres+26003.70%$260

Popular MLB futures odds

Both casual and die-hard baseball bettors can wager on their beloved sport all year round. Here's a look at the most popular MLB futures odds we cover at Sportsbook Review:

  • World Series Odds
  • World Series MVP Odds
  • MLB MVP Odds
  • MLB Rookie of the Year Odds
  • Cy Young Award Odds
  • Home Run Derby Odds
  • MLB Win Totals

How to use our MLB odds tables

Whether you're a rookie bettor eager to understand MLB odds tables, or a seasoned player aiming to maximize your wagering experience beyond perusing our MLB best bets, we've got everything you need right here.

Our MLB odds page showcases top-rated sportsbooks in your area, offering the most up-to-date odds for every game and futures market. Want to gain an instant advantage with MLB betting? Keep an eye out for odds highlighted in blue—they mark the best value for a particular wager.

Each set of game odds includes three important additional features:

  • The Favorite feature (indicated by a star to the left of the team names) lets bettors prioritize specific matchups on their main Odds page; simply click the star, and that game becomes a favorite.
  • The Matchup feature (indicated by the word "Matchup" to the right of the star) is a stathead's delight, transporting interested bettors to a page fully devoted to trends, comparisons and other game details for the game in question. Best of all, the Matchup page includes the best values for spread, total and moneyline plays.
  • The Odds History feature (indicated by parallel wavy lines to the right of the Matchup link) takes bettors through every major odds development since the lines first opened. Players can alternate between a variety of sportsbooks and quickly identify every time the spread or total shifted.

How to read MLB odds

MLB odds might look overwhelming to newcomers, but we’re here to simplify betting on one of the most popular sports leagues. Here’s a quick guide to understanding MLB odds:

In moneyline betting, you'll typically see a favorite (the team expected to win) and an underdog (the team expected to lose). The favorite will have odds with a minus sign, like -150. This means you need to bet $150 to win $100, as the favorite is considered a safer pick. On the other hand, if the underdog has +125 odds, a $100 bet would win you $125, since it’s a riskier play according to oddsmakers.

When it comes to run lines and totals, you'll often see odds at -110, which is common for MLB bets considered more of a toss-up. In this case, you would wager $110 to win $100, and the payout is the same regardless of which side you pick. The reason the odds aren’t exactly even is due to the “vig” – the small cut sportsbooks take for accepting the bet.

For more details on moneylines, run lines, and totals, check out the next section.

How to Bet on MLB

MLB fans have 2,430 regular-season games and a full postseason slate, culminating with the World Series. Every game offers countless betting opportunities, from pre-game to in-game markets. Here’s a breakdown of the main types of MLB bets:

Run Line Betting

The run line is MLB’s version of the point spread. It’s typically set at 1.5 runs to balance the odds between two teams. If you bet on the favorite at -1.5, they must win by 2 or more runs for your bet to cash. Conversely, if you bet on the underdog at +1.5, they just need to lose by 1 run or win outright.

Moneyline Betting

Moneyline betting is straightforward: you pick the team you think will win the game, regardless of the margin. However, payouts vary. Betting on a heavy favorite will require a larger wager for a smaller return, while betting on an underdog offers higher payouts but with greater risk.

Our comprehensive guide to moneyline betting answers a lot of additional questions novice players might have about this bet type.

Totals (Over/Under) Betting

In totals betting, you wager on whether the total number of runs scored in the game will be over or under the line set by the sportsbook. MLB also offers totals betting for individual team stats, such as total runs or strikeouts, and even player-specific totals.

Looking for more? Check out our guide to over/under betting.

Prop Betting

Prop bets focus on specific in-game outcomes rather than the overall result. You can bet on individual player performances (e.g., how many strikeouts a pitcher will get) or team milestones. With countless options per game, prop betting is ideal for fans who follow specific aspects of the sport closely.

Having access to multiple prop betting sites is an easy way to ensure that you're always getting the best MLB player props each day with the best odds for a given prop bet.

Parlay Betting

A parlay combines multiple bets into one, offering higher payouts but requiring all your selections to win. In MLB, you can parlay multiple games or even different bets within the same game (same-game parlays). While parlays are popular, they are also riskier, as one wrong outcome means the entire bet loses.

Our guide to parlay betting provides more info on parlay bet types, how to understand parlay odds, and strategies for parlay wagering.

Futures Betting

Futures bets allow you to wager on season-long outcomes, like picking the World Series champion or individual awards. These bets can be made months in advance, and many sportsbooks now offer the option to cash out early if the odds shift in your favor.

How to choose MLB betting sites

Knowing baseball is only part of what will make you a successful MLB bettor.

Just like making any important purchase, shopping around for the friendliest MLB odds can make a huge difference. Here are some tips to help you find the best MLB betting sites:

  • Prioritize quality of odds: You want access to sportsbooks that consistently offer the best MLB odds, with clear displays so you can easily compare prices and find the best value.
  • Leverage bonus offers: Take advantage of the best sportsbook promos, especially at the start of the MLB season and/or playoffs. BetMGM bonus code, FanDuel promo code and DraftKings promo code offers are among the strongest you'll find.
  • Consider sportsbook reputation: Selecting the right sportsbook can be daunting. Check their commitment to customer safety and responsible gaming. Our responsible gaming guide highlights how major sportsbooks prioritize player protection.
  • Trust expert reviews (like ours): If you're unsure about a sportsbook, let expert reviews guide you. Sportsbook Review has tested and rated dozens of top MLB betting sites, so whether you're keen on finding the best parlay betting sites or looking for a review of a top-tier platform like bet365, we have you covered.
